Ah, what a turn of events! After a tumultuous day fraught with haggling, and some rather unfortunate (for the bandits, that is) encounters, Janukovits relented and reduced his prices to a more acceptable figure. Geralt and Zoltan made quick work of the bandit issue - apparently, as Zoltan later told me, the bandits found themselves entirely outmatched when our steadfast friends sprung their trap from a merchant's cart.

ID: With that out of the way, all that was left was to organize everything for the grand party at the Chameleon.

And what a send-off it was, if I do say so myself. The Chameleon was overflowing with laughter, music, drink, and the mingling scents of delicate perfumes. A true night to remember for Geralt, Ciri, the sorceresses and of course me, and Priscilla. Even Avallach decided to join at some point.

After everything was said and done, I think we all felt a little more prepared—or at least a little more at peace - facing whatever comes next. I only hope my small contribution helped give everyone a moment of joy and respite before the trials continue.

EN: Come back to the Chameleon after 8. pm
Take care of business with Yen & Triss first

After all the trouble with Janukovits and the bandits, I had been so excited to host a grand farewell party for Geralt, Ciri, and the sorceresses here at the Chameleon. However, it seems that my well-laid plans have fell apart in the most disheartening way.

ID: Despite all our efforts, securing the supplies, arranging for food and drink, and even making sure the prices could be negotiated favorably, Geralt and his companions left for Skellige on a ship without even a word about the party.

Were they so pressed for time that a single night of revelry was out of the question? Did they forget? Or maybe, in the chaos of preparing for the battle against the Wild Hunt, they simply had other, more pressing matters demanding their immediate attention.
May the winds carry you safely to your destination. I only wished I could have given you a proper send-off. Maybe next time.

A Proper Send-off
A Proper Send-off

Well, here I am yet again, finding myself in a bit of a pickle. I had the most splendid idea to throw a grand farewell party for Geralt, Ciri, and the sorceresses - given that they're off to face the Wild Hunt. Such a momentous occasion calls for nothing less than an epic soiree, filled with music and the finest ales and vittles that Novigrad has to offer.

ID: But, alas, the fates seem intent on making my life difficult. I've been grappling with a rather droll suppier by the name of Janukovits Vobblewick, who seems to believe that now is the perfect time to inflate his prices. He also happens to sell the best wine around, among other things. Alas, his repeated hikes have made it nearly impossible to secure the necessary supplies for our soiree. So, naturally, I called upon Geralt for aid, as friends should.
Geralt agreed to help with this petty yet pressing matter. With Zoltan in tow, they went to negotiate with Janukovits at the market.
